metric_name: IQ/Objects object-oriented advantage vs frame-based reporters
metric_value: Unlike Cognos Impromptu objects, IQ/Objects can communicate between objects and pass attributes; delivers multiple views of multiple queries
Top-notch frame-based reporter; can give multiple views of a query within single report but lacks three-tier architecture
Competitor in frame-based reporting; has not yet delivered three-tier architecture as of 1996

metric_name: Three-tier architecture requirement for WWW BI
metric_value: Decision-support implementations grow 3-5x every 18 months; three-tier architecture required to avoid scalability/performance problems
IQ has had a Unix and NT three-tier architecture since 1995; one year of refinement gives it advantage over would-be competitors
WWW-enabled applications will require sophisticated three-tier architectures; will create a flurry of announcements among suppliers
Three-tier architecture became the standard for web-based enterprise applications and BI tools by 2000-2005; prediction proved accurate
notes: Modern web application architecture universally uses three-tier or n-tier patterns

metric_value: IQ Software must maintain current pace of innovation to maintain leadership position in WWW-enabled enterprise reporting

notes: Tech Monitor: Information Advantage completed acquisition Sept 1998

metric_value: HTML interface works poorly with Microsoft OLE; relatively primitive application development; Phase II IQ/LiveWeb will bypass HTML via browser plug-in

metric_name: ODBC as enterprise data connectivity standard

Two publishing modes: scheduled reporting for production/repetitive users; on-demand printing for time-sensitive non-standard information
SmartObjects have inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation; enable reusable query building blocks and parent/child reporting

metric_name: WWW BI requirement: knowledge base metadata management
metric_value: System must automatically import metadata, re-import if database schema changes, transform tables/columns into business terms

metric_name: WWW BI requirement: cross-database hyperlink navigation
metric_value: Users must be able to navigate across heterogeneous databases and share results; links must remain active when documents distributed
source_page: Bridging The Desktop With Cyberspace section

metric_name: WWW BI requirement: scalability for growth
metric_value: Successful decision-support implementations grow 3-5x every 18 months; architecture must handle this growth

metric_name: WWW-enabled enterprise BI as natural evolution
metric_value: WWW-enabled information-driven computing represents the natural evolution toward dynamic enterprise-class on-demand information access
Web-based BI became the dominant delivery model by 2000-2005; all major BI vendors (Cognos, Business Objects, MicroStrategy) shifted to browser-based delivery
notes: Proves Aberdeen's fundamental architectural prediction correct
